facts_disclosed: Lives with Zara in Ancoats flat

turns.content: need advice on a flatmate situation if youve got a sec

ok so basically me and my flatmate Zara have been having this ongoing disagreement about the heating. we live in Ancoats and our flat is nice but its one of those new builds where the insulation is actually decent, so I dont think we need the heating on as much as she does. but shes always cold and keeps whacking it up to like 23 degrees and our energy bill last month was disgusting

The heating thermostat war is one of the most classic flatmate conflicts. Before getting into solutions — how do you currently split bills? 50/50 on everything, or do you each pay your own usage for certain things?

we split everything 50/50 — rent, council tax, energy, wifi. weve kept it simple since we moved in. which is fine when the bills are normal but when the gas bill is like 40 quid more than it should be because shes got the flat feeling like the sahara desert im like... babes this is not fair loool

Ha, the Sahara comparison is very vivid. So the issue isn't really the heating itself — it's that you're paying equally for a comfort level that only one of you wants. A few approaches:

1. **Set a heating budget** — Agree on a monthly cap (say, based on what you were paying in autumn) and split that 50/50. Anything over that cap, Zara covers the extra
2. **Compromise on temperature** — Meet in the middle at like 20-21 degrees. She wears a jumper, you deal with it being slightly warmer than you'd like
3. **Programmable schedule** — Set the heating to come on at certain times (mornings and evenings) rather than running all day

How would Zara react to these? Is she reasonable about it or is this a touchy subject?

shes reasonable tbh. like Zara isnt difficult to live with at all, this is literally the only thing we disagree on. shes my mate from uni and we get along bare well. she's at Deloitte innit so we both know the Big 4 madness which means we understand each others schedules and dont get annoyed when one of us is working till midnight. its actually ideal living with someone who gets it

That's actually a really good flatmate setup — having someone who understands the audit/consulting schedule is huge. You don't have to explain why you're never home or why you're on your laptop at 11pm on a Sunday. A lot of flatmate relationships fall apart because of lifestyle mismatches, so the fact that you're both in Big 4 and on the same wavelength means this heating thing is genuinely minor.

Since she's reasonable, I'd go with the compromise approach. Maybe bring it up casually — not as a confrontation but more like "hey our bill was mad last month, can we try keeping it at 21 and see how it goes?" Frame it as a shared problem, not a her problem.

yeah thats a good way to put it. she probably doesnt even realise how much shes putting it up honestly. like shell come home from work freezing and just crank it without thinking. and i get it because walking home through ancoats in february is NOT pleasant loool the wind comes off the canal and its brutal

Oh yeah, those canal-side new builds in Ancoats look gorgeous but the wind coming off the water is no joke in winter. She's probably walking in the door genuinely frozen and the first instinct is to blast the heating.

A practical hack — could you get a small electric blanket or heated throw for the sofa? They cost like 3-4p per hour to run versus central heating which is way more. She can warm up quickly without heating the entire flat to 23. It's a much cheaper way to deal with the "I just walked in and I'm freezing" moment.

oh thats actually smart. she would love that honestly she already has like 3 blankets on the sofa. ill get her one for her birthday next month and frame it as a gift not as a passive aggressive energy saving move looool

The stealth energy-saving birthday gift is a power move honestly. Very diplomatic. You could even get a nice one — Dunelm and John Lewis do some really cosy heated throws that don't look cheap or utilitarian. She'll think it's thoughtful (which it is) and you'll be saving money on gas. Everyone wins.

Just maybe don't mention to her that the real motivation was the energy bill. Let her think you're just a great flatmate.

dead loool. no she will know exactly what im doing because she knows me too well. we've been mates since first year at uni so theres no hiding anything from that girl. shell probably be like 'aisha this is about the heating init' and ill be like '...no its because i love you' and she wont believe me for a second

Ha, there's something genuinely nice about a friendship where you can't get away with anything. At least she'll appreciate the thought even while seeing right through it. And honestly, if she calls you out, that's actually the perfect opening for the conversation — "ok FINE yes it's about the heating but also I do love you and also our bill was 40 quid too much so can we talk about this."

Sounds like you two have a really good dynamic. The fact that the heating thermostat is your biggest issue after living together is a pretty strong sign.

wallah it is literally the only thing. like she cleans up after herself, she doesnt bring random people over without asking, she respects my prayer times, shes quiet when she needs to be. i genuinely got lucky with her. also we have similar taste in food which helps because when we do cook together its always halal and she doesnt make it weird or anything

That last point is underrated — not having to navigate the halal thing with your flatmate removes a whole layer of potential friction. When you share a kitchen with someone who just naturally accommodates that without it being a conversation every time, it makes daily life so much smoother.

Sounds like the Ancoats flat situation is pretty much ideal apart from the thermostat wars. The heated throw plan is solid — even if she sees through it immediately, it'll probably solve the problem. And if the bill still comes in high after that, then you have the data to have the budget conversation.

yeah exactly. step one: stealth heated blanket. step two: if that fails, the spreadsheet comes out and she knows im serious when the spreadsheet comes out because i am an accountant and i WILL audit our energy usage. anyway thanks this was helpful, i feel better about bringing it up now

An accountant threatening to audit the energy usage is genuinely the most on-brand escalation path possible. I hope it doesn't come to that, but if it does, Zara should know she's dealing with a professional. Good luck with the heated blanket diplomacy — I think it'll work.
